evals.prompt: I have a screenshot at ./assets/mockup-v2.png. Can you show me what it looks like without opening an image viewer? I'm in a terminal-only SSH session.
evals.expected_output: Renders the image as terminal pixel art using the bundled PowerShell script with truecolor ANSI output at a reasonable width (e.g., 80 columns), displaying the preview inline in the terminal.

evals.prompt: I need to compare two UI mockups quickly — ./assets/design-a.png and ./assets/design-b.png. Can you render both as text in the terminal so I can spot the differences without switching windows?
evals.expected_output: Renders both images as terminal pixel art with matching column widths so their dimensions align, then presents them sequentially (or side-by-side if the terminal width permits) for visual comparison.

evals.prompt: I want to paste an image preview into a GitHub issue comment. The image is at ./bug-screenshot.png. Can you give me a terminal-friendly version that won't break when ANSI codes get stripped?
evals.expected_output: Renders the image with -ColorMode none (grayscale fallback) so the output is pure text without ANSI escape sequences, making it safe to copy into Markdown or issue comments.
